title: Classify Polygons by Their Attributes
essential_question: How can you classify polygons by the attributes they share?

pretest.question: Which group contains ONLY polygons with 4 sides?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Find the group where every shape has exactly 4 sides.

feedback.correct: Yes! Square, rectangle, and rhombus all have 4 sides — they share that attribute.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is B. Square, rectangle, and rhombus are all 4-sided polygons.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! Square, rectangle, and rhombus all have 4 sides — they share that attribute.

pretest.question: Which attribute do a square and a rectangle SHARE?
choices: A) All sides are the same length

A.note: Squares have all equal sides, but rectangles do not

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Look at the corners — what kind of angles do squares and rectangles share?

feedback.correct: Yes! Both squares and rectangles have 4 right angles.
feedback.incorrect: Both squares and rectangles have 4 right angles. That is the shared attribute.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! Both squares and rectangles have 4 right angles.

pretest.question: How many right angles does a square have?

0.note: All 4 corners of a square are right angles

2.note: All 4 corners are right angles, not just 2

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Each corner of a square is a square corner.

feedback.correct: Right! All 4 corners of a square are right angles.
feedback.incorrect: A square has 4 right angles — every corner is a square corner.

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! All 4 corners of a square are right angles.

pretest.question: Which polygon has ALL sides the same length?

B.note: A non-square rectangle has 2 long sides and 2 short sides

C.note: Trapezoids do not need equal sides

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Which shape has every side the same length?

feedback.correct: Yes! A square has all 4 sides equal.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is A. A square has all sides equal in length.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! A square has all 4 sides equal.

pretest.question: Which attribute do a triangle and a hexagon BOTH have?
choices: C) Closed figures with all straight sides

B.note: Most triangles and hexagons have no right angles

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Both are polygons — closed with straight sides.

hints: What is true for every polygon, no matter the number of sides?

feedback.correct: Yes! Both are polygons — closed figures with straight sides.
feedback.incorrect: All polygons share this: closed figures with all straight sides. Triangles and hexagons both qualify.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! Both are polygons — closed figures with straight sides.

learn.title: What Is an Attribute?
learn.content: An ATTRIBUTE is a special feature of a shape — like number of sides, kind of angles, or whether sides are equal. Examples of attributes: 'has 4 sides', 'has a right angle', 'has all sides equal'. We can sort shapes into groups by their attributes.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Classify Means Sort into Groups
learn.content: To CLASSIFY shapes is to put them in groups by something they have in common. Example: Put all polygons with 3 sides in one group, and all polygons with 4 sides in another group. The 3-sided group has triangles. The 4-sided group has squares, rectangles, rhombuses, and trapezoids.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Classify by Number of Right Angles
learn.content: Another way to classify polygons is by their angles. SQUARE: 4 right angles. RECTANGLE: 4 right angles. RIGHT TRIANGLE: 1 right angle. REGULAR PENTAGON: 0 right angles. So squares and rectangles share the attribute '4 right angles'.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Classify by Side Lengths
learn.content: We can sort polygons by whether their sides are equal. ALL SIDES EQUAL: square (4 equal sides), rhombus (4 equal sides), equilateral triangle (3 equal sides). NOT ALL SIDES EQUAL: rectangle that is not a square, scalene triangle, most trapezoids.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Shapes Can Have Many Attributes
learn.content: One shape can have many attributes at the same time. A SQUARE has: 4 sides, 4 right angles, all sides equal. So a square belongs to several groups: 'has 4 sides', 'has 4 right angles', and 'has all sides equal'. The same shape can fit into more than one group.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Some Groups Live Inside Other Groups
learn.content: Every SQUARE is also a RECTANGLE — because a square has 4 sides and 4 right angles, just like rectangles. But not every rectangle is a square (a rectangle that is 5 cm by 3 cm has 4 right angles but is not a square). So the 'square group' fits INSIDE the 'rectangle group'.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Two Shapes Share an Attribute When…
learn.content: Two shapes SHARE an attribute when both shapes have that feature. Square and rectangle both have 4 right angles. Square and rhombus both have 4 equal sides. Triangle and hexagon both are closed figures with all straight sides (both are polygons). Look for what they have in common.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today you learned: (1) An ATTRIBUTE is a feature of a shape. (2) To CLASSIFY is to sort shapes into groups by attributes. (3) Common attributes: number of sides, number of right angles, equal side lengths. (4) One shape can have many attributes. (5) A square is also a rectangle, because every square has 4 right angles.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Sort by number of sides. Which group contains only TRIANGLES?
choices: A) Equilateral triangle, scalene triangle, right triangle
solution_steps: Step 1: A triangle is any polygon with 3 sides.
Step 2: Equilateral, scalene, and right triangles all have 3 sides.
Step 3: They are all triangles.

B.note: Those are all quadrilaterals, not triangles

D.note: Square=4 sides, pentagon=5 sides — not all triangles

feedback.correct: Yes! All three are triangles — every one has 3 sides.
feedback.incorrect: Triangles have 3 sides. Choice A — equilateral, scalene, right triangle — all qualify.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: How many right angles does a rectangle have?
solution_steps: Step 1: A rectangle has 4 corners.
Step 2: Every corner is a right angle.
Step 3: 4 right angles total.

0.note: A rectangle's corners are right angles

feedback.correct: Right! A rectangle has 4 right angles.
feedback.incorrect: A rectangle has 4 right angles — every corner is a square corner.

try.question: Which attribute do a SQUARE and a RHOMBUS share?
solution_steps: Step 1: Square has 4 equal sides.
Step 2: Rhombus has 4 equal sides.
Step 3: Both share 'all sides equal'.

A.note: Square has 4 right angles; a typical rhombus does not

feedback.correct: Yes! Both have all sides equal — that is what they share.
feedback.incorrect: Both square and rhombus have all 4 sides equal in length. That is the shared attribute.

try.question: Which of these is true about a SQUARE?
choices: C) All its sides are equal
solution_steps: Step 1: A square has 4 sides — TRUE.
Step 2: A square has 4 right angles — TRUE.
Step 3: A square has all sides equal — TRUE.
Step 4: All three are correct.

A.note: True, but B and C are also true

B.note: True, but A and C are also true

C.note: True, but A and B are also true

feedback.correct: Right! A square has many attributes — 4 sides, 4 right angles, and all sides equal.
feedback.incorrect: All three are true for a square. A square has many attributes at once.

practice_r3.question: Anna says, 'My polygon has 4 sides AND all sides equal.' Which polygon could Anna's NOT be?
choices: C) A rectangle that is not a square
D) A square tilted on its corner
solution_steps: Step 1: Check each shape: 4 sides AND all sides equal?
Step 2: Square — 4 sides, 4 equal sides. Fits.
Step 3: Rhombus — 4 sides, 4 equal sides. Fits.
Step 4: Rectangle (not square) — 4 sides but NOT all equal. Does NOT fit.

D.note: A tilted square is still a square

feedback.correct: Right! A non-square rectangle has 2 long and 2 short sides — sides are not all equal.
feedback.incorrect: A rectangle that is not a square does not have all equal sides. So it cannot be Anna's polygon.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What two rules does Anna's polygon follow? Read 2: Which choices fit both rules? Read 3: Which choice fails one rule?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Test each shape against BOTH rules.
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! A non-square rectangle has 2 long and 2 short sides — sides are not all equal.

practice_r3.question: From this list — triangle, square, circle, hexagon, open V-shape, rectangle — how many are polygons?
solution_steps: Step 1: Triangle — closed, all straight. Polygon.
Step 2: Square — closed, all straight. Polygon.
Step 3: Circle — curved. NOT a polygon.
Step 4: Hexagon — closed, all straight. Polygon.
Step 5: Open V-shape — not closed. NOT a polygon.
Step 6: Rectangle — closed, all straight. Polygon.
Step 7: Total = 4.

6.note: Circle is curved, V-shape is not closed

feedback.correct: Right! Triangle, square, hexagon, rectangle = 4 polygons.
feedback.incorrect: 4 polygons. Circle (curved) and open V (not closed) are NOT polygons.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What is the list? Read 2: What two tests must each pass to be a polygon? Read 3: How many pass both tests?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Apply both polygon tests to each item: closed? all straight?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! Triangle, square, hexagon, rectangle = 4 polygons.

practice_r3.question: Ben has a 4-sided polygon with 4 right angles. Which statement MUST be true?
choices: C) It is a rectangle (or a square)
D) All sides are different lengths
solution_steps: Step 1: 4 sides AND 4 right angles defines a rectangle.
Step 2: All squares are rectangles (they also have 4 right angles).
Step 3: So it must be a rectangle (which includes squares as a special case).

A.note: MUST is too strong — sides could be unequal

B.note: Rhombuses do not need to have right angles

D.note: Rectangles can have 2 pairs of equal sides

feedback.correct: Right! 4 sides + 4 right angles = rectangle (a square is a special rectangle).
feedback.incorrect: It must be a rectangle. Squares are rectangles, but it is not GUARANTEED to be a square.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What two attributes does Ben's polygon have? Read 2: Which shapes always have those attributes? Read 3: Which choice must be true?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons

hints: What shape is defined by 4 sides AND 4 right angles?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! 4 sides + 4 right angles = rectangle (a square is a special rectangle).

practice_r3.question: True or False: Every rhombus is a square.

true.note: Rhombuses do not all have right angles, so not all are squares

feedback.correct: False! A rhombus has 4 equal sides but does not need 4 right angles.
feedback.incorrect: False. A rhombus may not have right angles. So not every rhombus is a square.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What is a rhombus? Read 2: What does a square need? Read 3: Does every rhombus have those?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Squares need right angles; rhombuses do not.

hints: Does every rhombus have right angles?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: False! A rhombus has 4 equal sides but does not need 4 right angles.

practice_r3.question: Which statement is TRUE about polygons?
choices: A) Every square is a rectangle
B) Every rectangle is a square
C) Every triangle has 4 right angles
D) Every polygon has equal sides
solution_steps: Step 1: Squares have 4 sides + 4 right angles → that fits the rectangle definition.
Step 2: Rectangles need not have all sides equal — not all are squares.
Step 3: Triangles have 3 angles, not 4.
Step 4: Most polygons do not have equal sides.

B.note: Squares are rectangles, but not all rectangles are squares

D.note: Most polygons do NOT have equal sides

feedback.correct: Right! Every square is a rectangle (4 sides + 4 right angles). The square group lives inside the rectangle group.
feedback.incorrect: A is true: every square is a rectangle. The other statements are false.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What does each statement say? Read 2: Test each one with examples. Read 3: Which is always true?
